message:
fix(hook): unpin tests from /data/projects; satisfy nightly clippy

CI was red across every platform: the `hook::tests` module was using
`tempfile::tempdir_in("/data/projects")` in 22 places, which only works
on the maintainer's dev host. Every CI runner (macos-x64, macos-arm64,
linux-x64, linux-arm64, coverage) panicked at tempdir creation with
`NotFound: "/data/projects/.tmpXXXXXX"`, failing 23-25 tests each.

Introduces a `topology_tempdir()` helper in the tests module that
creates a platform-portable tempdir via `tempfile::tempdir()`, then
builds a `PathTopologyPolicy` whose canonical root is the
canonicalized tempdir path and whose alias root is a sibling sentinel
that intentionally does not prefix any real path (so
`normalize_project_path_with_policy` doesn't try to verify it as a
symlink). Every test that previously pinned to `/data/projects` now
uses this helper plus the returned policy instead of
`PathTopologyPolicy::default()`.

Also tightens the two `/dp` alias tests (`test_canonicalize_dp_alias`
and `test_plan_primary_via_dp_alias_canonical`) to guard on
`fs::canonicalize` success rather than `Path::exists`, since on CI the
`/dp` workflow step creates `/dp` as a plain directory containing only
`rich_rust`, so `Path::exists("/dp")` returns true but
`canonicalize("/dp/remote_compilation_helper")` rightly fails.

The `test_execute_remote_compilation_syncs_custom_cargo_target_dir_artifacts`
test now chdirs into the tempdir before calling
`execute_remote_compilation` and restores the previous cwd after the
call, because that function reads the project root from
`std::env::current_dir()` and normalizes it through the supplied policy.

Adjusts the two e2e tests' "should be rejected" sentinel paths to use
`/etc`, `/usr/local`, `/opt`, and `/var/empty` subpaths so the
rejection check doesn't overlap with the tempdir's own parent on Linux
(`/tmp/...`) or macOS (`/var/folders/...`).

Separately, fixes three pre-existing clippy errors the same CI run was
flagging under `-D warnings` on `nightly-2025-11-01`:

- `rch-common/src/cargo_path_deps.rs`: two collapsible-if patterns
  rewritten as `if ... && let ...`.
- `rch-common/src/patterns.rs`: nested `if` inside a `match` arm
  collapsed into a match guard.
- `rch/src/hook.rs`: one collapsible-if in `build_dependency_runtime_plan`
  rewritten as a single `if let ... && seen.insert(...)`; one
  `.map(|v| { ...; v })` replaced with `.inspect(|_| { ... })`; one
  `filter_map(|x| cond.then(|| ...))` rewritten as `filter(...).map(...)`.

All refactors are semantically equivalent — no user-visible behaviour
change, so no version bump.

Resolves all 23 macos-arm64 `hook::tests::` failures in CI #24529328213
plus the matching failures on the other four test matrix jobs and the
coverage job. Also unblocks the `fmt` job (trailing whitespace cleanup
in `rchd/src/health.rs`) and the `clippy` job.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
